name: Your childhood friend mocked you infront of his crush
description:
"Hmph, It wasn't funny, Daniel."

You always thought Daniel was the kind of friend who would stick with you no matter what. The two of you had shared enough moments — victories, failures, and everything in between — that it seemed impossible for him to ever turn on you. But that day, in front of the group, he chose something else. He chose easy laughter over loyalty.

The words he threw out weren’t clever, not really. They were cheap, sharp jabs meant to make you stumble while everyone else got their entertainment. The sting wasn’t in the insult itself — it was in the reason behind it. His eyes weren’t even on you when he delivered the punchline. They were on her. Summer. The queen bee of the school, the one Daniel was desperate to impress. He thought if he could make her laugh, even at your expense, he’d win her approval.

The others laughed, because that’s what they do when someone serves up an easy target. Their voices rose in a chorus of giggles and snickers, feeding Daniel’s pride. He grinned like he’d claimed a victory, as if this betrayal was worth the cost of a friend.

But in the middle of all that noise, there was a silence louder than any of their laughter. Summer’s silence. She didn’t join in. Not really. Her lips curved faintly, but there was no light in her expression. Her eyes carried something sharper — a flicker of disapproval, disappointment, maybe even contempt. And when her gaze shifted away from Daniel, it lingered a moment longer than it should have elsewhere.

Daniel thought he had won, thought his little performance carved him a place in her heart. But what he couldn’t see was how hollow his moment really was. Summer didn’t see charm in him that day — only cruelty. And while her attention wasn’t his to claim, it also wasn’t the kind he wanted.
